2026-04-08T19:53:16.1791805Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m

2026-04-08T19:56:13.8170954Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-08T19:54:21.340589392Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57945,\"title\":\"[ConfidentialLedger] Docs: Document redirect policy behavior and fill in Examples section\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/`\\n**Triggered by:** [REDACTED] by `@ryazhang-microsoft`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nThe commit cached the primary ACL node URL inside `ConfidentialLedgerRedirectPolicy` to speed up write operations. When the service issues a 307/308 redirect pointing to the primary node, the policy now stores that target and sends subsequent non-GET requests directly to it — bypassing the load balancer round-trip. The cache is invalidated on transport failures or 5xx responses so the client can re-discover the primary node automatically.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The README **Key concepts** section has no mention of the SDK\\u0026#39;s transparent redirect-following behavior (introduced in 1.4.1-beta.3, enhanced in 1.4.1-beta.5). Users who observe cross-node routing or performance differences have no documentation to explain what the SDK is doing automatically.\\n- The redirect caching optimization — and its automatic invalidation on node failures — is not documented anywhere for end users.\\n- The **Examples** section of the README contains only `Coming Soon...` with no working code examples at all.\\n- The CHANGELOG captures all these changes but the README has not been brought up to date.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/README.md`\\n**Action:** Edit\\n\\n1. In the **Key concepts** section, add a new sub-section **Redirect and primary-node routing** after the existing sub-sections:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Redirect and primary-node routing\\n\\nAzure Confidential Ledger is a distributed system where write operations must be processed by the primary node. Non-primary nodes respond to write requests with an HTTP 307 or 308 redirect to the current primary. The SDK\\u0026#39;s built-in redirect policy (`ConfidentialLedgerRedirectPolicy`) handles these redirects transparently:\\n\\n- Redirects are followed automatically while preserving the `Authorization` header, which is required because ACL nodes are all trusted within the same ledger.\\n- After the first successful redirect, the primary node\\u0026#39;s URL is cached inside the client. Subsequent write requests are sent directly to the cached primary, avoiding an extra round-trip through the load balancer.\\n- The cache is automatically invalidated when a transport failure or a 5xx response is received from the cached primary, allowing the client to re-discover the primary node through the normal redirect flow.\\n\\nThis behavior is transparent — no configuration is required. Users with write-heavy workloads benefit automatically from reduced latency once the primary node has been identified.\\n```\\n\\n2. Replace the **Examples** section content:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\nThe following examples demonstrate common operations with Azure Confidential Ledger.\\n\\n### Append a ledger entry\\n\\n```\\nOperation postOperation = ledgerClient.PostLedgerEntry(\\n    waitUntil: WaitUntil.Completed,\\n    RequestContent.Create(\\n        new { contents = \\u0026#34;Hello world!\\u0026#34; }));\\n\\nstring transactionId = postOperation.Id;\\nConsole.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Appended transaction with Id: {transactionId}\\u0026#34;);\\n```\\n\\n### Get transaction status\\n\\n```\\nResponse statusResponse = ledgerClient.GetTransactionStatus(transactionId);\\n\\nstring status = JsonDocument.Parse(statusResponse.Content)\\n    .RootElement\\n    .GetProperty(\\u0026#34;state\\u0026#34;)\\n    .GetString();\\n\\nConsole.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Transaction status: {status}\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n// Wait for the entry to be committed\\nwhile (status == \\u0026#34;Pending\\u0026#34;)\\n{\\n    statusResponse = ledgerClient.GetTransactionStatus(transactionId);\\n    status = JsonDocument.Parse(statusResponse.Content)\\n        .RootElement\\n        .GetProperty(\\u0026#34;state\\u0026#34;)\\n        .GetString();\\n}\\n\\nConsole.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Transaction status: {status}\\u0026#34;);\\n```\\n\\n### Get a receipt for a transaction\\n\\n```\\nResponse receiptResponse = ledgerClient.GetReceipt(transactionId);\\nstring receiptJson = new StreamReader(receiptResponse.ContentStream).ReadToEnd();\\n\\nConsole.WriteLine(receiptJson);\\n```\\n```\\n\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** The snippet placeholders (`Snippet:AppendToLedger`, `Snippet:GetStatus`, `Snippet:GetReceipt`) already exist in the existing README so they do not need to be added — but an explicit **Examples** intro and section headings should be added to give each snippet meaningful context.\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nThe existing README already references snippet regions `AppendToLedger`, `GetStatus`, and `GetReceipt` that are backed by test files. No new snippet regions need to be created for this documentation update. However, if a new snippet demonstrating the redirect behavior is desired, the following approach should be used:\\n\\n1. **Test file:** `sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/tests/Samples/` — create or extend an existing sample class with a method that performs a write operation; the redirect policy behavior is automatic.\\n2. **README placeholder:** Add `` ```C# Snippet: `` followed by a closing `` ``` `` at the desired location in the README.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe current README contains these required sections but some are incomplete:\\n\\n| Section | Status |\\n|---|---|\\n| Getting started | ✅ Present and populated |\\n| Key concepts | ⚠️ Missing redirect/primary-node routing sub-section |\\n| Examples | ❌ Empty (\\u0026#34;Coming Soon...\\u0026#34;) |\\n| Troubleshooting | ✅ Present |\\n| Next steps | ✅ Present |\\n| Contributing | ✅ Present |\\n\\nReference: `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md`\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 confidentialledger`\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 confidentialledger`\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/confidentialledger/Azure.Security.ConfidentialLedger/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt;
